codeReview.summary: The fix addresses both prior blockers: SDK packaging now emits and exports true CJS/ESM entrypoints (including @cash/shared for CJS dependency resolution), and EventIndexer.poll now merges cross-type events and sorts by (transaction_version, event_index) before processing, which resolves the stale-depth ordering path identified in the prior review.

issues.description: New ordering tests do not exercise EventIndexer.poll() (the path that merges and sorts RPC events); they call processEvent directly, so regressions in fetch/merge/sort logic could still pass tests. Coverage proves order-dependent state behavior, but not the poll-level ordering implementation itself.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill mandates TDD red-phase ('Write tests FIRST' then run failing tests), but this worker session ran baseline passing tests and performed implementation edits before adding the new ordering tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl:26 shows `cd .../sdk && pnpm test` and `cd .../api && pnpm test` before edits, then later edits to api/src/indexer/event-indexer.ts before adding new tests in api/src/indexer/event-indexer.test.ts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature expected behavior references `node -e require('@cash/orderbook-sdk')`, but practical verification in this repo used path-based checks (`require('./dist')` and requiring sdk by absolute package directory). The local validation convention for package-name runtime checks is still unclear in shared guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json (fix-sdk-cjs-indexer-ordering expectedBehavior) requires package-name require, while handoff verification commands use `cd sdk && ... require('./dist')`.

summary: Review result: pass. This fix resolves the two original blocking failures (SDK CJS runtime compatibility and cross-type indexer ordering) and includes added regression tests, with one non-blocking test-coverage gap noted for poll-path ordering assertions.
